steps.title: Connect the discriminant to the number of solutions
steps.content:
The question asks how many distinct real solutions the quadratic has. For a quadratic, the sign of the discriminant tells whether the graph crosses the $x$-axis twice, touches it once, or does not cross it. For $x^2-12x+27=0$:
$$(-12)^2-4(1)(27)=144-108=36$$ Since $36\gt0$, the quadratic has two distinct real solutions.

steps.title: Interpret the discriminant
steps.content: The positive discriminant means the parabola crosses the $x$-axis at two different points. Therefore the equation has exactly two distinct real solutions.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The correct answer says there are two distinct real solutions. The other choices do not match the positive discriminant, so A is correct.
